top_signals.title: US Stocks Slip Ahead of CPI and Warsh as IBM Sinks: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: US stocks slipped as traders refrained from taking on risk on a busy day that kicked off with Wall Street earnings. Testimony from Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh and inflation data are due later.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Deutsche Bank May Win Russia Sanctions Lawsuit Against Linde
top_signals.summary: Deutsche Bank AG may win a legal battle in a dispute with a corporate client over who has to pick up the tap for losses linked to sanctions on Russia, according to a German court.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: IBM shares drop more than 17% after company warns second-quarter earnings fell short of expectations
top_signals.summary: CEO blamed the shortfall on weakness in the software and infrastructure business because clients shifted money toward hardware purchases.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: World Cup's biggest spenders show up late as semifinals drive host city travel boom
top_signals.summary: World Cup travel demand to U.S. host cities is spiking as the tournament reaches the semifinal stage.

top_signals.title: Infantino facing IOC probe after complaint lodged over Trump’s Balogun intervention
top_signals.summary: Fifa boss Gianni Infantino is facing an International Olympic Committee investigation after an official complaint was filed following US President Donald Trump’s intervention to block Folarin Balogun’s ban. The decision to suspend Balogun’s ban – enabling the USA’s best player to continue playing in the World Cup after he was sent off against Bosnia and [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
